name: Red Bell Pepper Picante with Rice, Beans and Turkey
recipeIngredient: 2 tablespoons low sodium chili sauce, or catsup
1/2 cup lowfat chicken broth, low salt
1 large red bell pepper, cored for stuffing
1 large yellow bell pepper, cored for stuffing
1 cup cooked navy beans, rinsed and drained
1 cup shredded cabbage, napa or savoy
1 can stewed red ripe tomatoes, Italian seasoned
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut an 'x' into both ends of the onion. Place in a wok. Cover with water (about 2 cups) and bring to a boil; boil 3 to 4 minutes with one end down, then flip, and boil 2 to 3 minutes on the other. Remove the onion to a collander and cool under cold water. Reserve the onion water in a bowl with a spout.
2. Put the dried chili peppers in that hot water and set aside.
3. Chop the onion.
4. Dry wok with paper toweling; heat oil in wok; saute mushrooms, bell pepper, and onion over medium to medium-high until mushrooms are soft and fragrant.
5. Add the ground turkey to the wok's center, crumble and stir fry until no longer pink.
6. Increase heat; add madeira and deglaze.
7. Add tomatoes, chili sauce or catsup, and broth. Bring to a gentle boil.
8. Add garlic. Reduce heat and add seasonings: fennel seeds, rubbed sage, dried rosemary, mint flakes, hot sauce, parsley, and cilantro.
9. Add optional grated orange rind. Moisten with some of the reserved onion water, as desired.
10. Steam the peppers until crisp tender (7 to 10 minutes depending on microwave or steamer). Test after 5 minutes; do not let them get too soft.
11. Preheat oven to 375F. Layer a small casserole with shredded cabbage (Chinese, napa or savoy).
12. Stuff each pepper and stand upright in the casserole. Distribute the leftover stuffing around the peppers.
13. Bake covered for 15 minutes. Then bake uncovered for 10 mins.
14. SAUCE: Put the two sauce ingredients in the wok. Bring to a boil; reduce heat to medium, medium-low and simmer (15 minutes). If the sauce reduces too much, thin with remaining onion water. Optional: put the serrano chilies in the sauce.
15. Serve: plate half a pepper, 1 crown broccoli and some sauce. Have extra shredded cabbage on hand.
